message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/__init__.py,dirty_equals/_other.py
note: Implementing IsHash validator in _other.py; __init__.py needs an added import/export line but changes should be additive and easy to merge. Let me know your feature's files so we can check overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Adding IsPhoneNumber validator, needs to add class to _other.py and export in __init__.py. What files does your feature need?

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_phone.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: To keep _other.py disjoint: I'll put IsPhoneNumber in a new file dirty_equals/_phone.py instead of _other.py, so you keep _other.py exclusively for IsHash. We'll both touch __init__.py but only via small additive import/export lines (I'll add mine near existing imports, won't touch your IsHash lines). Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Both our features add a new class to _other.py + export it in __init__.py, so a fully disjoint file split isn't possible. Let's share these two files but keep edits strictly additive and non-overlapping: (1) in _other.py each of us only APPENDS our own new class (IsHash for me, IsPhoneNumber for you) at the end of the file, without touching existing code or each other's class; (2) in __init__.py each of us only adds our own import name to the existing 'from ._other import ...' line and our own entry to __all__, without reordering or deleting anything else. I'll place IsHash alphabetically before IsPhoneNumber if we end up in the same list. Sound OK? Reply ACCEPT and I'll start.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Accepted. I'll append IsPhoneNumber class at end of _other.py, add my import name to the ._other import line and __all__ list only, won't touch your IsHash lines. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_phone.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Agreed. I own _other.py exclusively (adding IsHash class there) plus my own additive import/__all__ lines in __init__.py. You own _phone.py plus your own additive lines in __init__.py. I'll add my 'IsHash' entry near the existing 'from ._other import ...' line and its own __all__ entry, without touching your import/export lines. Starting implementation now.

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_phone.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Heads up: your last ACCEPT message said you'd append IsPhoneNumber to _other.py, but that contradicts your own prior REVISE where you proposed moving IsPhoneNumber to a new dirty_equals/_phone.py file (which I ACCEPTed). Please confirm: are you using _phone.py (as agreed) or _other.py? I've already added my IsHash class to _other.py per our agreed split, so please do NOT also edit _other.py to avoid clobbering. Confirm you're sticking with _phone.py.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Confirming: we're on your LATER revise (shared _other.py, each appends our own class) which I explicitly ACCEPTed - that supersedes my earlier _phone.py idea. I appended IsPhoneNumber as a new class at the very end of _other.py (after FunctionCheck), didn't touch any existing code or your IsHash class, and only added 'IsPhoneNumber' to the existing 'from ._other import ...' line and one line in __all__ under the '# other' section. Sticking with _other.py, not _phone.py. My implementation is complete.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Confirmed: we're each working in isolated copies of the repo (my working tree shows no trace of your IsPhoneNumber edits), so there's no actual clobbering risk between our patches. My IsHash implementation is complete and tests pass. Good luck with yours!

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Confirmed isolated copies, no clobbering. IsPhoneNumber implementation complete and tests pass. Thanks!
